articles.title: Rochdale Weather Forecast: Warm and Sunny Days Ahead
articles.excerpt: Rochdale is set to experience warm and sunny weather over the coming days, with temperatures reaching up to 23°C.
articles.content_html:
A warm and sunny weather pattern is expected in Rochdale over the next few days. Today, temperatures are forecasted to reach a high of 23°C, with minimal chance of rain and a light breeze from the northeast.

On Friday, the temperature will remain similar, also reaching 23°C, while Saturday will see a slight drop to 21°C with sunny intervals and a 20% chance of rain in the afternoon.

The forecast for Sunday indicates a return to 22°C, maintaining the trend of warm weather. Pollen levels are expected to be high throughout this period, which may affect those with allergies.

Looking ahead to next week, temperatures will continue to hover around 22°C, with sunny intervals and a low chance of rain. Residents should stay hydrated and enjoy the pleasant weather.

articles.title: Rochdale In Bloom Hosts North West in Bloom Judges
articles.excerpt: Rochdale In Bloom welcomed judges from North West in Bloom to the town centre this week, showcasing various local projects including Broadfield Slopes and the living roof at Number One Riverside.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale In Bloom welcomed judges from North West in Bloom to the town centre this week. The event showcased several local projects, including Broadfield Slopes, the living roof at Number One Riverside, Baillie Street Quarter, and the town hall square.

The judges assessed the efforts made in enhancing the town's green spaces and overall appearance. This initiative aims to promote community involvement and environmental sustainability in Rochdale.

Local volunteers and community groups contributed to the projects highlighted during the judges' visit. Their efforts reflect a commitment to improving the town's landscape and fostering community pride.

The results of the North West in Bloom competition will be announced later this year, with hopes that Rochdale will receive recognition for its contributions to the initiative.

articles.content_html:
The weather forecast for Castleton, Rochdale, on July 16, 2026, predicts a mix of sun and clouds with occasional rain showers expected.

Temperatures are likely to reach a high of 20 degrees Celsius, while overnight lows may drop to around 12 degrees Celsius.

People should carry umbrellas as rain is expected intermittently throughout the day, particularly in the afternoon.

For those planning travel or outdoor activities, it is advisable to check for updates on weather conditions as they may change.

articles.title: UK Faces Unprecedented Climate Change with Extreme Temperatures
articles.excerpt: The UK is experiencing unprecedented climate change, with extreme temperatures expected to persist in the coming years. Experts warn that the impacts of climate change are increasingly evident across the country.
articles.content_html:
The UK is experiencing unprecedented climate change, with extreme temperatures expected to persist in the coming years. Experts warn that the impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly evident across the country.

Recent reports indicate that the frequency and intensity of heatwaves are likely to increase, posing challenges for public health and infrastructure.

In Rochdale, residents may notice changes in weather patterns, which could affect local services and community activities.

The ongoing climate crisis highlights the need for collective action to address environmental issues and promote sustainability within communities.

articles.title: Twelve Rochdale borough green spaces retain Green Flag Awards
articles.excerpt: Twelve parks and green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines have received Green Flag Awards for another year.
articles.summary: Rochdale Borough Council has celebrated Green Flag Award success at 12 local green spaces, praising staff and volunteers who maintain the parks throughout the year.
articles.content_html:
Twelve parks and green spaces across Rochdale borough are celebrating another year of Green Flag Award success.

The recognised sites are located across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines.

Rochdale Borough Council described the awards as a significant achievement for the employees and volunteers who work throughout the year to keep the borough’s green spaces welcoming and accessible.

Recognition for well-managed green spaces

The Green Flag Award is an international quality mark for parks and green spaces.

Sites are assessed against standards covering areas such as cleanliness, environmental management, biodiversity, safety, community involvement and the quality of their facilities.

The awards recognise spaces that are well managed and provide benefits for residents, visitors and local wildlife.

Thanks to staff and volunteers

The council praised the teams and community volunteers who contribute to the upkeep of local parks throughout the seasons.

Their work includes maintaining paths, gardens and planted areas, caring for habitats and helping to ensure the spaces remain pleasant places for recreation and relaxation.

“A fantastic achievement for our teams and volunteers who work year-round to create welcoming spaces that offer so many benefits for residents and visitors to our borough.”

Residents are being encouraged to look out for Green Flags flying at their local award-winning park or green space.

This report is based on a public announcement and photographs published by Rochdale Borough Council following the latest Green Flag Award results.
articles.event_location: Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines
articles.right_to_reply: Rochdale Borough Council, Keep Britain Tidy, local park groups and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Rochdale Borough Parks Achieve Green Flag Awards

articles.content_html:
Rochdale borough parks have received Green Flag awards, a mark of excellence in park management and maintenance. This recognition highlights the quality of the parks across the borough.

The Green Flag awards are given to parks that meet strict criteria, including cleanliness, safety, and environmental management. The initiative encourages the management of parks and green spaces to provide a welcoming environment for visitors.

Local officials expressed pride in the achievement, noting that the awards reflect the hard work of staff and volunteers who maintain these spaces.

The Green Flag status is likely to enhance the appeal of Rochdale's parks, encouraging more people to enjoy outdoor activities and community events.

articles.title: Firefighters Tackle Moorland Fire Near Dovestone Reservoir
articles.excerpt: Firefighters are working to control a grass fire near Dovestone Reservoir, with smoke affecting areas across Saddleworth and parts of Rochdale.
articles.content_html:
Firefighters are battling a grass fire on open moorland near Dovestone Reservoir, which has sent large plumes of smoke across Saddleworth. The Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service was called just before 8.45pm on Saturday to reports of the fire.

Five fire engines from Mossley, Stalybridge, Hyde, Bolton North and Littleborough attended the scene, supported by specialist wildfire units and officers. Crews located the fire and used grass beaters and backpack water units to tackle the flames while working to prevent it from spreading further across the moor.

Residents in Saddleworth and neighbouring areas of Rochdale reported seeing smoke drifting across the skyline. Some also reported hearing loud bangs before the fire became visible, although there is no confirmation that these sounds were connected to the incident.

The cause of the fire has not yet been confirmed, and firefighters remained at the scene late into the evening. The fire service has advised residents and visitors in the area to keep doors and windows closed while smoke remains present. Anyone travelling near Dovestone Reservoir is advised to avoid the area while emergency services continue their work.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Norden Village Summer Fete to feature duck race and family entertainment
articles.excerpt: Norden Village Summer Fete will take place on Sunday 19 July, featuring a duck race with cash prizes, family attractions, food, music and stalls.
articles.summary: The summer fete will begin at St Paul’s Parish Hall in Norden at 1pm, with the village duck race starting at 2pm.
articles.content_html:
Norden Village will host its Summer Fete on Sunday 19 July 2026, with an afternoon of family entertainment, food, music and the return of the village duck race.

The event will begin at 1pm at St Paul’s Parish Hall on Blackpits Road.

The duck race will start at 2pm, with cash prizes of £250 for first place, £100 for second place and £50 for third place.

Ducks cost £5 to enter and are available to purchase from participating businesses around Norden Village.

Visitors can also enjoy a bouncy castle, face painting, an ice cream van, a sweet stall, a bar, live music, food and other activities throughout the afternoon.

Event details

  • Date: Sunday 19 July 2026
  • Start time: 1pm
  • Duck race: 2pm
  • Location: St Paul’s Parish Hall, Blackpits Road, Norden
  • Duck entry: £5

Organisers have advised that there will be no parking available at the event and are asking local visitors to leave their cars at home where possible.

The fete is hosted by the Norden Environmental and Economic Village Association, known as NEEVA. Organisers have also stated that the duck race will not cause environmental harm.
articles.image_alt: Poster advertising Norden Village Summer Fete and duck race on Sunday 19 July
articles.image_caption: Norden Village Summer Fete will begin at 1pm, with the duck race taking place at 2pm.
articles.image_credit: Norden Environmental and Economic Village Association
articles.event_location: St Paul’s Parish Hall, Blackpits Road, Norden, Rochdale
articles.source_name: Norden Environmental and Economic Village Association

articles.content_html:

Council members emphasised the importance of community involvement in these initiatives, encouraging residents to participate in upcoming events to help improve their local environment.

Further details about the initiatives and how residents can get involved will be shared on the council's official website and social media channels.

articles.title: Concerns Raised Over Abandoned Turner Brothers Factory in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Site owners warn of increased trespassing at the derelict Turner Brothers factory, known as Rochdale's Chernobyl, amid summer holidays and ongoing safety risks.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 11 July 2026 at 15:33. 3 source updates have been combined into this article.

Site owners warn of increased trespassing at the derelict Turner Brothers factory, known as Rochdale's Chernobyl, amid summer holidays and ongoing safety risks.

Latest update

Site owners warn of increased trespassing at the derelict Turner Brothers factory, known as Rochdale's Chernobyl, amid summer holidays and ongoing safety risks.

A site in Rochdale known as the Turner Brothers factory has raised safety concerns among local residents. The factory, often referred to as Rochdale’s Chernobyl, has been surrounded by high fences for years, intended to deter trespassers.

However, these barriers have proven insufficient in protecting the site from outsiders. Reports indicate that the abandoned factory remains accessible, leading to fears about potential hazards associated with the derelict building.

Local authorities have been urged to take further action to secure the site and address the risks it poses to the community. The factory's deteriorating condition has sparked discussions about its future and the need for a comprehensive safety assessment.

Earlier developments

Anyone with information regarding the site or concerns about safety is encouraged to reach out to local authorities.

A site known as Rochdale's Chernobyl is facing increased trespassing issues as summer holidays approach. The Turner Brothers factory, once the world's largest asbestos manufacturer, is now abandoned and off-limits to the public. Site owners, Spodden Park Ltd, have reported a rise in trespassers, particularly during school holidays, with security alerted over 100 times in October 2025 alone.

Experts warn that asbestos fibres still present in the decaying buildings pose significant health risks to those who enter. Spodden Park Ltd has urged parents to remind their children of the dangers associated with the site, which includes the risk of asbestos exposure and potential injuries from collapsing structures. The company has produced a factsheet detailing the health hazards linked to asbestos, including asbestosis and lung cancer.

Last year, the number of trespassers increased by 25 per cent during school holidays, with estimates of 30 to 60 breaches per month. Locals have reported seeing children playing in the River Spodden, which flows through the factory site. Spodden Park Ltd has stated that entering the site requires breaking through a fence, which constitutes a crime, and has encouraged residents to report any such incidents to the police.

The site owners continue to monitor the area and work with security services to maintain safety. They have expressed concerns that emergency services would struggle to reach anyone injured within the fenced-off site. As the summer holidays begin, Spodden Park Ltd is preparing for a potential spike in trespassing incidents and reiterating the importance of safety awareness.

Traffic disruptions have been reported near the abandoned Turner Brothers factory in Spotland. The site, often referred to as Rochdale’s Chernobyl, has been surrounded by high fences for years, intended to deter trespassers.

Despite these barriers, the factory remains a point of concern for local authorities due to safety issues. The area has attracted attention from both the public and local officials, who are aware of the potential hazards posed by the derelict building.

Motorists are advised to exercise caution when travelling through the vicinity, as the disruptions may affect journey times. Local traffic management is monitoring the situation and may implement further measures if necessary.

Update timeline

  • 11 July 2026, 15:33: Concerns Raised Over Abandoned Turner Brothers Factory in Rochdale
  • 11 July 2026, 15:33: Traffic Disruption Near Abandoned Turner Brothers Factory in Spotland

articles.title: Rochdale's Chernobyl Site Faces Increased Trespassing Concerns
articles.excerpt: Landowners of the abandoned Turner Brothers factory site in Rochdale, known as 'Rochdale's Chernobyl', report a rise in trespassing incidents, particularly during school holidays, raising safety concerns.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 11 July 2026 at 07:38. 3 source updates have been combined into this article.

Landowners of the abandoned Turner Brothers factory site in Rochdale, known as 'Rochdale's Chernobyl', report a rise in trespassing incidents, particularly during school holidays, raising safety concerns.

Latest update

A derelict factory site in Rochdale, referred to as 'Rochdale's Chernobyl', is experiencing rising concerns over trespassing incidents. The abandoned Turner Brothers factory, once the world's largest asbestos manufacturer, is located in a residential area just north of Rochdale town centre.

Landowners Spodden Park Ltd have reported a significant increase in trespassers, particularly during school holidays. In October 2025 alone, security was alerted to trespassing incidents more than 100 times, with estimates of 30 to 60 breaches per month, representing a 25 per cent rise during school holidays. Locals have also observed children playing in the River Spodden, which flows through the factory site.

The site remains off-limits to the public due to hazardous conditions, including the presence of asbestos fibres. Spodden Park Ltd has urged parents to educate their children about the dangers of entering the site, highlighting the risks of asbestos exposure and potential injuries from collapsing structures. They have produced a factsheet detailing the health risks associated with asbestos, including asbestosis and lung cancer.

As summer approaches, Spodden Park Ltd anticipates further increases in trespassing, particularly among local teenagers. They continue to monitor the site and work with security services to ensure safety, advising residents to report any illegal entry to the police.

Earlier developments

Concerns have been raised regarding the exclusion zone established around Rochdale's Chernobyl site. Local residents fear that the measures in place may not be sufficient to ensure safety in the area.

The exclusion zone was implemented to limit access to the site, which has been a source of environmental concern. However, some residents believe that the current restrictions are not being enforced effectively.

Residents have reported instances of people entering the zone, raising questions about the adequacy of safety protocols.

Authorities have been urged to take action to address these concerns.

Update timeline

  • 11 July 2026, 07:38: Rochdale's Chernobyl Site Faces Increased Trespassing Concerns
  • 11 July 2026, 07:38: Concerns Over Exclusion Zone at Rochdale's Chernobyl Site
